items.question: Which statement best describes a multilayer perceptron?
options: A recurrent network with memory of past inputs
A feedforward network with at least one hidden layer and nonlinear activations between linear maps
A single threshold unit without hidden tiers
A convolutional network for image data only
items.answer: A feedforward network with at least one hidden layer and nonlinear activations between linear maps
items.explanation: An MLP stacks hidden layers (linear + nonlinearity) before the output head in a feedforward path — no recurrence or convolution required.

items.question: Why is XOR impossible for a single perceptron but learnable by a small MLP?
options: XOR requires more training epochs than a perceptron allows
XOR is not linearly separable; hidden units create nonlinear features the output can combine
XOR needs softmax on hidden layers
Perceptrons cannot use bias terms
items.answer: XOR is not linearly separable; hidden units create nonlinear features the output can combine
items.explanation: No single hyperplane separates XOR classes. Hidden ReLU (or similar) units form intermediate regions the output layer merges into the correct boundary.

items.question: What happens if you stack three nn.Linear layers with no activation between them?
options: The network learns three independent decision boundaries
The stack is equivalent to one linear layer — no extra nonlinear capacity
Training automatically inserts ReLU
Only the middle layer receives gradients
items.answer: The stack is equivalent to one linear layer — no extra nonlinear capacity
items.explanation: Composition of linear maps is linear. Nonlinear activations at hidden tiers are required for non-linear decision boundaries.

items.question: For MNIST (10 classes), which output layer matches standard cross-entropy training?
options: nn.Linear(hidden, 10) producing logits
nn.Linear(hidden, 1) with sigmoid
nn.Linear(hidden, 10) followed by ReLU
Ten separate binary models without a shared hidden stack
items.explanation: CrossEntropyLoss expects one logit per class. Hidden tiers use ReLU; the output stays linear logits.

items.question: What is the primary role of hidden layers in an MLP?
options: To store the training dataset
To apply nonlinear feature recombination before the output head
To replace the need for a loss function
To convert regression problems into classification automatically
items.answer: To apply nonlinear feature recombination before the output head
items.explanation: Hidden layers transform inputs into internal representations that the output layer maps to labels or scores — enabling non-linear boundaries.
